authority_posture.proof: Lean source checked by the pinned Lean kernel
authority_posture.navigation: generated projections and this descriptor; not proof authority
authority_posture.semantic_bridge: approximate correspondence only; never a proof edge
authority_posture.methodology: docs/methodology.json defines mathematical methodology and claim-transition requirements; it is not proof authority or claim-status authority

premise_chain.boundary: The public atlas does not claim elaborator-derived declaration dependencies or accessibility annotations.

status_taxonomy.proved here: Lean theorem in the pinned public release; no novelty assertion is implied
status_taxonomy.formalised here: Known mathematics represented and checked in Lean
status_taxonomy.unconditional progress: Genuine theorem that does not settle the open problem
status_taxonomy.conditional reduction: Exact implication whose remaining hypothesis is open
status_taxonomy.verified finite instance: Kernel-checked computation at a bounded range
status_taxonomy.cited only: External theorem used for positioning, not formalised

claims.label: Full-support Erdős–Borwein irrationality, every integer base b ≥ 2
claims.statement: For every integer b ≥ 2, the series ∑_{n≥1} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational (Erdős, 1948).

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality, unconditional.** For every base `b ≥ 2` the series `∑_{n≥1} 1/(b^n - 1)` is irrational — Erdős (1948), through the certificate machine: bounded Bertrand/CRT first-block frame, middle-window divisor-pair average with pigeonhole selection, and explicit parameter closure.

claims.label: The Erdős–Borwein constant E is irrational
claims.statement: E = ∑_{n≥1} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) is irrational, as the base-2 corollary of the full-support theorem.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosBorwein_series
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosBorwein_series : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **The Erdős–Borwein constant is irrational.** Base-2 instance of the full-support theorem: `∑_{n≥1} 1/(2^n - 1)` is irrational.

claims.label: Named infinite-support irrationality families toward #257
claims.statement: For every base b ≥ 2, ∑_{n∈A} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational for factorial support, power-of-two support, multiples, pairwise-coprime supports with summable reciprocals, eventually-periodic supports, residue classes, and the odd numbers. Proved families, not the universal statement.

declarations.name: erdos257_family_factorial_instance
declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_factorial_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (Nat.factorial (k + 1)) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #257 family, factorial instance at base 2.** The literal series `∑ 1 / (2^(n!) - 1)` over the infinite support `A = {n! : n ≥ 1}` is irrational. This is an instance of the statement family of Erdős problem #257 (which asks the same for *every* infinite support); the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

declarations.name: erdos257_family_two_pow_instance
declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_two_pow_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (2 ^ k) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #257 family, geometric instance at base 2.** The literal series `∑ 1 / (2^(2^n) - 1)` over the infinite support `A = {2^n : n ≥ 0}` is irrational. An instance of the #257 statement family; the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hA : A.Infinite) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hsum : Summable (Set.indicator A fun a : ℕ => (1 : ℝ) / a)) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős's pairwise-coprime support theorem (1948), machine-checked.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every infinite pairwise-coprime support `A ⊆ ℕ` with summable reciprocals, `∑_{a ∈ A} 1/(b^a - 1)` is irrational. The first non-dilation support class of Erdős #257 to submit to the support-coefficient calculus: this is NOT valuation forcing — small support elements join the CRT modulus at residue zero, adaptive block sizes absorb their deterministic first-block hits, and summable reciprocals control the strays.

claims.label: The Mersenne–Lambert ladder identities
claims.statement: L(μ) = 1/2, L(φ) = 2, L(φ*μ) = S, and the Möbius-square lens S = 1/2 + ∑ μ(d)/(2^d − 1)², placing the open constant one convolution from the machine-checked irrational E.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(φ) = 2`**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2^d-1) = 2`, exactly. The Lambert transform of the #249 coefficients at the #249 point is rational — the difficulty of #249 lives entirely on the power-series side.

declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens**: `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ = 1/2 + ∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d-1)²`. The #249 constant as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series.

claims.label: Unconditional denominator exclusion for the totient constant
claims.statement: S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is not equal to p/q for any integer p and any 1 ≤ q ≤ 79 639 646 646 701 375 323 355 774 875 831 053 (≈ 7.96 × 10³⁴). A lower bound on any rational representation, not a proof of irrationality.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `79639646646701375323355774875831053`, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `79639646646701375323355774875831053`.

claims.label: Exact reduction of #249 to finite decidable certificates
claims.statement: If certified non-integrality witnesses occur at unbounded parameters (equivalently on the lcm diagonal), then S is irrational. The unboundedness of the supply is the remaining open hypothesis.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-21 reduction (the throat of #249).** A certified period-kill supply — for every period `h ≥ 1` and every threshold `N₀`, a certified kill at some `(N ≥ N₀, L)` — proves the totient series irrational. Each individual certificate is a finite, decidable residue computation.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse.** #249 follows from a SINGLE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements: the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (periodLcm t, periodLcm t)` for infinitely many `t`. Rationality would supply a primitive period `h₀` and pre-period `N₀`; any `t ≥ max(h₀, N₀)` gives `h₀ ∣ periodLcm t` and `periodLcm t ≥ t ≥ N₀` simultaneously, so a diagonal kill at such `t` is a contradiction. No second parameter remains.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law.** If the totient series is rational, then from some scale on, the ENTIRE lcm cone is fractionally flat: every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer, for every `t` past the threshold, every base multiplier `q ≥ 1`, and every step multiplier `m`. One constant fractional part governs every cone point — the maximal spend of the rationality hypothesis on the lcm lattice.

claims.label: Certificate completeness
claims.statement: A certified kill exists at fixed parameters exactly when the corresponding tail difference is non-integral, so the finite computation loses no information.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) ↔ totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness, iff form.** Some depth certifies `(h, N)` exactly when the tail difference is a non-integer. Finite certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

claims.label: Initial certified-kill segment and generated certificates
claims.statement: Kernel-checked certified kills for small periods and for the lcm diagonal up to t = 8, plus machine-generated finite certificate shards, each discharged by decide.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_all_small
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_all_small : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, certifiedKill h 12 16
declarations.docstring: Machine-checked kills: for every period `h ∈ [1,8]` the residue certificate holds at `(N, L) = (12, 16)`. Kernel-decidable: 256 totient values below 37.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven : certifiedKill (periodLcm 7) (periodLcm 7) 14
declarations.docstring: `P 7`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..7) = 420`, depth `14`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight : certifiedKill (periodLcm 8) (periodLcm 8) 15
declarations.docstring: `P 8`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..8) = 840`, depth `15`.

claims.bounded_domain: The explicitly listed small periods, the lcm diagonal through t = 8, and the parameters encoded by the generated certificate shards.

claims.label: Tempered tail-orbit rigidity criterion
claims.statement: For coefficients c(n) ≤ n, the binary series ∑ c(n)/2ⁿ is rational exactly when a tempered integer carry orbit exists, and every tempered orbit equals the scaled tail.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: **Subexponential tail-orbit rigidity, equivalence form.** The series `X_c` is rational exactly when some tempered integer orbit exists.

claims.label: Boolean–Möbius carry certificate equivalence
claims.statement: A nonempty support with series value p/q exists exactly when a positive, square-root-bounded integer orbit with Boolean Möbius quotient exists; the support is reconstructed from the certificate.

declarations.name: exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry
declarations.signature: theorem exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) : (∃ A : Set ℕ, 0 ∉ A ∧ (∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)) ↔ ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U
declarations.docstring: **Certificate-level equivalence.** Positive normalized supports with value `p/q` are in exact correspondence at the existence level with quotient-only Boolean Möbius carry certificates. The converse support is not guessed: it is reconstructed as `booleanMobiusSupport` of the carry quotient.

claims.label: Rationality-forced reciprocal-mass lower bound
claims.statement: A rational support-series value with odd denominator part v forces reciprocal mass at least 1/ord_v(2), or divergence; an infinite support with dyadic-rational value has reciprocal mass divergent or greater than one.

declarations.name: one_div_oddOrder_le_reciprocalMass_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem one_div_oddOrder_le_reciprocalMass_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.toNat.Coprime v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : (1 : ℝ) / (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A

claims.label: Carry states are unbounded over infinite rational-valued supports
claims.statement: An infinite support with rational series value has an unbounded positive carry state, closing finite-state readings of the carry system.

declarations.name: exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hAinf : A.Infinite) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℕ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p.toNat * 2 ^ n [MOD v]) ∧ (∀ B : ℕ, ∃ n : ℕ, B < u n)
declarations.docstring: Canonical fraction-facing unboundedness theorem. Every infinite rational support admits the existing shifted natural tail state, and that state is globally unbounded.

claims.label: Sublogarithmic divisor-coverage zero windows
claims.statement: A rational support-series value forces every zero window in the divisor-count coefficient to have length at most ε·log₂(N+1) + O_{ε,c,v}(1), uniformly in the support. This constrains coverage; it does not solve universal #257.

declarations.name: supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ B : ℝ, 0 ≤ B ∧ ∀ N h : ℕ, SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h → (h : ℝ) ≤ ε * Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ) + B
declarations.docstring: For every positive `ε`, a rational support-series value forces every support-coefficient zero window to have length at most `ε * log₂(N+1)` plus a constant depending only on `ε`, `v`, and `c`. The bound is uniform in the support `A`, numerator `p`, starting index `N`, and window length `h`.

claims.label: Greedy geometry of the Mersenne achievement set
claims.statement: Membership in the Mersenne achievement set is equivalent to greedy survival at every level; finite exact-rational death certificates soundly prove non-membership (3/4 is excluded at level one). The underlying geometry is recorded by Kovač–Tao.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival (x : ℝ) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet ↔ 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: **Greedy membership criterion.** A real target belongs to the Mersenne achievement set exactly when it is nonnegative and every residual is at most the full remaining tail.

declarations.name: three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.signature: theorem three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet : (3 / 4 : ℝ) ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Consequently `3/4` is not in the Mersenne achievement set.

claims.label: Transcendence of ∑ σ(m)/2^m
claims.statement: Nesterenko (1996); the ladder rung L(Id). Cited for positioning, not formalised here.

claims.label: Irrationality of the prime-support series ∑_p 1/(2^p − 1)
claims.statement: Tao–Teräväinen; settles the prime-support special case of #257. Only the identity bridge to ∑ ω(n)/2ⁿ is formalised, not the irrationality.

claims.label: Erdős #249: irrationality of S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ
claims.statement: Not proved by this release. The reductions convert it into the unboundedness of the certificate supply, which is untouched.

claims.label: Erdős #257, universal form
claims.statement: Irrationality of ∑_{n∈A} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) for every infinite A is not proved by this release; only named families are formalised.

non_claims.meaning: This release does not settle the universal Erdős #257 problem
This release does not settle Erdős #249
This release does not itself constitute a peer-reviewed publication record
This release is not asserted to be equivalent to any private development
Provider or model output is not proof authority; only the Lean kernel check is
No unpublished proof sketch or hidden artefact carries proof authority for this release

remaining_open_propositions.statement: Prove that S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is irrational, with no bound on a possible rational denominator.
Produce certified non-integrality witnesses at unbounded parameters, equivalently along the lcm diagonal.
Prove irrationality for every infinite support A, not only the named support families formalised here.

nodes.role: Coprime-pair interpretation of the totient series
Assembled theorem kernel and headline interfaces
Greedy geometry and finite death certificates
Residue wraps, reciprocal mass, and unbounded carries

edge_semantics.corollary_of: The source claim is presented as a corollary or specialization of the target claim.
edge_semantics.builds_on: The source claim's argument layer builds on the target claim or criterion.
edge_semantics.makes_exact: The source claim records why a reduction or certificate formulation loses no information.
edge_semantics.instantiates: The source claim supplies checked finite instances of the target reduction.
edge_semantics.advances_open_target: The source claim is honest partial progress toward the open target and does not settle it.
edge_semantics.positions: The source claim is cited or used to position another claim; it is not formal proof authority here.

remaining_open_effect.statement: Proves the claim for named infinite-support families while leaving arbitrary infinite supports open.
Excludes every rational representation up to the stated denominator bound without proving irrationality.
Re-expresses the open irrationality step as the unbounded supply of checked finite certificates.
Adds a necessary reciprocal-mass condition for a rational value over an infinite support.
Adds a necessary sublogarithmic zero-window condition for a rational value over an infinite support.
Provides exact finite non-membership tests for the value set without proving the universal irrationality statement.

human_capsule: The exact boundary between proved and open must remain explicit.
Lean checks the stated proposition; it does not check that the proposition expresses the intended mathematics.
A public claim is transferred between representations only through a proved implication or equivalence.
A reduction must state the hypothesis or quantifier that remains open.
A finite result remains finite unless a theorem supplies the unbounded or universal step.
Progress toward an open problem must state which outstanding obligation it proves, narrows, decomposes, re-expresses, or constrains.

change_classes.summary: A proof body changes; the stated proposition does not
change_classes.public_consequence: No claim or exposition change follows automatically. Compare the axiom audit before and after.

change_classes.summary: A stated proposition or its hypotheses change
change_classes.public_consequence: Reconcile the claim wording, the remaining-open boundary, and the paper statement.

change_classes.summary: The transitive assumptions of a declaration change
change_classes.public_consequence: Reassess the proof posture even when the proposition is unchanged.

change_classes.summary: A claim moves between coordinates, encodings, or models
change_classes.public_consequence: Record the transport theorem, its direction, and anything not preserved.

change_classes.summary: A checked bounded range or certificate family is extended
change_classes.public_consequence: Keep the finite status; update only the stated bound.

change_classes.summary: A claim's declaration coordinates or links are corrected; the claim is otherwise unchanged
change_classes.public_consequence: Link or correct the supporting declarations of an unchanged claim, then regenerate dependent projections. The mathematical claim does not change; a Lean check confirms the linked declaration exists and elaborates.

change_classes.summary: An advances-open-target relation is added or changed
change_classes.public_consequence: Record the outstanding obligation affected and the exact relation.

change_classes.summary: The status of a public claim changes
change_classes.public_consequence: State the theorem or review that licenses the new status.

change_classes.summary: Authored paper or documentation prose changes mathematically
change_classes.public_consequence: Prose must not exceed the status recorded in the claim registry.
Regenerate below the existing owners; no mathematical effect.
Regenerate METHODOLOGY.md and the corpus descriptor. Rules route review; they do not decide mathematics.

human_review_triggers.description: The wording of a public claim changes.
The status of a public claim changes.
The reviewed correspondence between a proposition and its intended statement changes.
What the repository says a result means for an open problem changes.
The meaning of a methodology rule changes, not only its wording or projection.

forbidden_effect_vocabulary.description: The change must not alter the status of an open target claim.
The change must not derive an unbounded or universal statement from bounded evidence.
The change must not strengthen the wording of any public claim.
The change must not present a transformed representation as the original claim without a checked transport theorem.
The change must not edit a generated projection except by regenerating it from its owner.

high_salience_declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality, unconditional.** For every base `b ≥ 2` the series `∑_{n≥1} 1/(b^n - 1)` is irrational — Erdős (1948), through the certificate machine: bounded Bertrand/CRT first-block frame, middle-window divisor-pair average with pigeonhole selection, and explicit parameter closure.

high_salience_declarations.name: irrational_erdosBorwein_series

high_salience_declarations.docstring: **The Erdős–Borwein constant is irrational.** Base-2 instance of the full-support theorem: `∑_{n≥1} 1/(2^n - 1)` is irrational.

high_salience_declarations.name: erdos257_family_factorial_instance
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_factorial_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (Nat.factorial (k + 1)) - 1))

This is an instance of the statement family of Erdős problem #257 (which asks the same for *every* infinite support); the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

high_salience_declarations.name: erdos257_family_two_pow_instance
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_two_pow_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (2 ^ k) - 1))
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Erdős #257 family, geometric instance at base 2.** The literal series `∑ 1 / (2^(2^n) - 1)` over the infinite support `A = {2^n : n ≥ 0}` is irrational. An instance of the #257 statement family; the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

high_salience_declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hA : A.Infinite) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hsum : Summable (Set.indicator A fun a : ℕ => (1 : ℝ) / a)) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)

The first non-dilation support class of Erdős #257 to submit to the support-coefficient calculus: this is NOT valuation forcing — small support elements join the CRT modulus at residue zero, adaptive block sizes absorb their deterministic first-block hits, and summable reciprocals control the strays.

high_salience_declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 2
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(φ) = 2`**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2^d-1) = 2`, exactly. The Lambert transform of the #249 coefficients at the #249 point is rational — the difficulty of #249 lives entirely on the power-series side.

high_salience_declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens**: `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ = 1/2 + ∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d-1)²`. The #249 constant as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series.

high_salience_declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `79639646646701375323355774875831053`, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `79639646646701375323355774875831053`.

high_salience_declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **The wave-21 reduction (the throat of #249).** A certified period-kill supply — for every period `h ≥ 1` and every threshold `N₀`, a certified kill at some `(N ≥ N₀, L)` — proves the totient series irrational. Each individual certificate is a finite, decidable residue computation.

high_salience_declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse.** #249 follows from a SINGLE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements: the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (periodLcm t, periodLcm t)` for infinitely many `t`. Rationality would supply a primitive period `h₀` and pre-period `N₀`; any `t ≥ max(h₀, N₀)` gives `h₀ ∣ periodLcm t` and `periodLcm t ≥ t ≥ N₀` simultaneously, so a diagonal kill at such `t` is a contradiction. No second parameter remains.

high_salience_declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law.** If the totient series is rational, then from some scale on, the ENTIRE lcm cone is fractionally flat: every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer, for every `t` past the threshold, every base multiplier `q ≥ 1`, and every step multiplier `m`. One constant fractional part governs every cone point — the maximal spend of the rationality hypothesis on the lcm lattice.

high_salience_declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) ↔ totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness, iff form.** Some depth certifies `(h, N)` exactly when the tail difference is a non-integer. Finite certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

high_salience_declarations.name: certifiedKill_all_small
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_all_small : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, certifiedKill h 12 16
high_salience_declarations.docstring: Machine-checked kills: for every period `h ∈ [1,8]` the residue certificate holds at `(N, L) = (12, 16)`. Kernel-decidable: 256 totient values below 37.

high_salience_declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalKillDepth t)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`.

high_salience_declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven : certifiedKill (periodLcm 7) (periodLcm 7) 14
high_salience_declarations.docstring: `P 7`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..7) = 420`, depth `14`.

high_salience_declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight : certifiedKill (periodLcm 8) (periodLcm 8) 15
high_salience_declarations.docstring: `P 8`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..8) = 840`, depth `15`.

high_salience_declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Subexponential tail-orbit rigidity, equivalence form.** The series `X_c` is rational exactly when some tempered integer orbit exists.

high_salience_declarations.name: exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) : (∃ A : Set ℕ, 0 ∉ A ∧ (∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)) ↔ ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Certificate-level equivalence.** Positive normalized supports with value `p/q` are in exact correspondence at the existence level with quotient-only Boolean Möbius carry certificates. The converse support is not guessed: it is reconstructed as `booleanMobiusSupport` of the carry quotient.

high_salience_declarations.name: one_div_oddOrder_le_reciprocalMass_of_support_fraction
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem one_div_oddOrder_le_reciprocalMass_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.toNat.Coprime v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : (1 : ℝ) / (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A

high_salience_declarations.name: exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hAinf : A.Infinite) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℕ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p.toNat * 2 ^ n [MOD v]) ∧ (∀ B : ℕ, ∃ n : ℕ, B < u n)
high_salience_declarations.docstring: Canonical fraction-facing unboundedness theorem. Every infinite rational support admits the existing shifted natural tail state, and that state is globally unbounded.

high_salience_declarations.name: supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ B : ℝ, 0 ≤ B ∧ ∀ N h : ℕ, SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h → (h : ℝ) ≤ ε * Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ) + B
high_salience_declarations.docstring: For every positive `ε`, a rational support-series value forces every support-coefficient zero window to have length at most `ε * log₂(N+1)` plus a constant depending only on `ε`, `v`, and `c`. The bound is uniform in the support `A`, numerator `p`, starting index `N`, and window length `h`.

high_salience_declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival (x : ℝ) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet ↔ 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
high_salience_declarations.docstring: **Greedy membership criterion.** A real target belongs to the Mersenne achievement set exactly when it is nonnegative and every residual is at most the full remaining tail.

high_salience_declarations.name: three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
high_salience_declarations.signature: theorem three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet : (3 / 4 : ℝ) ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
high_salience_declarations.docstring: Consequently `3/4` is not in the Mersenne achievement set.
